verses.text: She is 'the rose of Sharon,' and her beloved 'as the apple tree among the trees of the wood,' in whose shade she sits with delight. He surpasses all others. The believer finds in Christ a shelter and sweetness no other can give.

She is 'the rose of Sharon,' and her beloved 'as the apple tree among the trees of the wood,' in whose shade she sits with delight. He surpasses all others. The believer finds in Christ a shelter and sweetness no other can give.

She is 'the rose of Sharon,' and her beloved 'as the apple tree among the trees of the wood,' in whose shade she sits with delight. He surpasses all others. The believer finds in Christ a shelter and sweetness no other can give.

He brings her to the banqueting house, 'his banner over me was love'; faint with longing, she asks not to have love stirred 'till he please.' Love has its seasons. The soul feasted by Christ rests content under the banner of His love.

He brings her to the banqueting house, 'his banner over me was love'; faint with longing, she asks not to have love stirred 'till he please.' Love has its seasons. The soul feasted by Christ rests content under the banner of His love.

He brings her to the banqueting house, 'his banner over me was love'; faint with longing, she asks not to have love stirred 'till he please.' Love has its seasons. The soul feasted by Christ rests content under the banner of His love.

He brings her to the banqueting house, 'his banner over me was love'; faint with longing, she asks not to have love stirred 'till he please.' Love has its seasons. The soul feasted by Christ rests content under the banner of His love.

She hears her beloved's voice as he comes 'leaping upon the mountains,' calling, 'Rise up, my love... and come away.' His coming is joyful and swift. So Christ calls His people to rise and follow Him.

She hears her beloved's voice as he comes 'leaping upon the mountains,' calling, 'Rise up, my love... and come away.' His coming is joyful and swift. So Christ calls His people to rise and follow Him.

She hears her beloved's voice as he comes 'leaping upon the mountains,' calling, 'Rise up, my love... and come away.' His coming is joyful and swift. So Christ calls His people to rise and follow Him.
'For, lo, the winter is past,' the flowers appear, the birds sing, and the vines bloom. A new season has come. The voice of the beloved turns the soul's cold winter into the springtime of joy.
'For, lo, the winter is past,' the flowers appear, the birds sing, and the vines bloom. A new season has come. The voice of the beloved turns the soul's cold winter into the springtime of joy.
'For, lo, the winter is past,' the flowers appear, the birds sing, and the vines bloom. A new season has come. The voice of the beloved turns the soul's cold winter into the springtime of joy.

He calls his dove from the cliff to show her face, and bids them catch 'the little foxes, that spoil the vines.' Small sins ruin tender love. The faithful must guard against the little faults that mar communion with Christ.

He calls his dove from the cliff to show her face, and bids them catch 'the little foxes, that spoil the vines.' Small sins ruin tender love. The faithful must guard against the little faults that mar communion with Christ.
'My beloved is mine, and I am his,' she rejoices, until the day breaks and shadows flee. Belonging is mutual and sure. The covenant between Christ and His own is a settled, treasured bond.
